what_it_does: Renders small, deterministic visual previews (specimens) for design-system kinds using resolved variable values, a per-kind seed, and a variant index; it maps kind IDs and categories to specialized SVG/DOM preview components and exposes a global MS_VARY table to bias variant axes.
observations: Uses window.MS_VARY as a global, shared source for per-kind visual variant axes.
Implements a deterministic linear-congruential PRNG (spRand) seeded from kind id, seed, and variant.
Provides helper functions that normalize resolved tokens for color, radius, density, and warmth into CSS-friendly values.
KindSpecimen applies variant bias by forcing a different axis value for variants 1/2 when MS_VARY defines one.
Many Sp* components ignore resolved values and rely on seeded randomness for shape, layout, or state.
Falls back from specific kind IDs to category-based preview components when no explicit mapping exists.
References an external Icon component and many CSS custom properties that are not defined in this file.
standouts: The MS_VARY global coupling between the renderer and an external resolver.
Deterministic seeded visual randomness tied to kind identity and variant.
A large, handcrafted switchboard mapping kind IDs and categories to miniature preview components.
The variant-bias mechanism that tries to make the three options visibly distinct.
conventions: Functional React components with inline style objects.
Window globals for cross-module shared tables (MS_VARY, KindSpecimen).
Helper prefix sp* for specimen utilities and Sp* for specimen components.
CSS variable names follow a gold/bronze design-token palette.
Seeded randomness used as a substitute for missing resolved values in several components.
concerns: Reliance on window globals creates an implicit external contract not visible in imports.
When no MS_VARY entry exists, the variant branch returns {} instead of preserving base values, discarding resolved tokens.
Several preview components do not consume resolved values, weakening the resolution-driven claim.
Color normalization falls back silently for non-hex/rgb/hsl/var tokens.
The Icon component is used but not defined or imported in this file.
Magic numbers in the PRNG constants and seed formula are unexplained.
notes: A switchboard of tiny seeded React preview components that turn resolved design tokens into deterministic thumbnail visuals, with a global variant-bias table.
questions: Where is MS_RESOLVE defined and how does it populate the values prop?
Is the Icon component imported or globally provided elsewhere?
Which runtime CSS custom properties are required for the specimens to render correctly?
Should the variant fallback preserve base values instead of returning an empty object?
How are kind.category values assigned and kept in sync with the category fallback mapping?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to render resolution-driven visual previews per kind; the code does this for some kinds, but many preview components ignore resolved values and rely on seeded randomness, so the resolution-driven claim is only partially realized.
apparent_themes: Single shared tables as a source of truth
Seeded mock visuals for rapid exploration
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make design-system options visually distinguishable in a catalogue or live interview by giving each kind a seeded, variant-biased thumbnail that reflects its resolved variables while still showing three visibly different options.
novelty: The combination of a per-kind seeded PRNG, a global MS_VARY axis table shared with an external resolver, and a large handcrafted switchboard of miniature DOM/SVG preview components tuned to a specific token palette.
contribution: A self-contained specimen renderer that can produce stable, varied visual thumbnails for many design-system kinds from a small set of resolved values and a seed; the MS_VARY axis idea and deterministic preview components are the keepable pieces.
summary_for_embedding: JSX file implementing KindSpecimen and a family of Sp* preview components that render deterministic, seeded visual thumbnails for design-system kinds from resolved token values and variant biases.
intention_for_embedding: Provide resolution-driven, visually distinct preview specimens for a generative design catalogue and live interview tool.

symbols.does: Deterministic seeded linear-congruential PRNG.
symbols.description: Returns a closure that generates pseudo-random floats in [0,1) using fixed LCG constants, seeded by an integer. It makes (kind, seed, variant) previews stable but varied across the catalogue.

symbols.does: Selects a random element from an array using a supplied PRNG.
symbols.description: Consumes one value from the provided PRNG closure and returns the array element at the resulting index. It is used throughout specimen components to choose shapes, weights, and layouts.

symbols.does: Normalizes a resolved color token into a usable CSS color value.
symbols.description: Passes through hex, rgb, hsl, and CSS var(...) strings. For any other value it returns a fallback color, preventing unresolved names or tokens from producing invalid styles.

symbols.does: Converts a resolved radius token into a pixel radius.
symbols.description: Treats small numbers as multipliers scaled by 9, caps larger numbers at 22 px, and maps named tokens crisp/balanced/rounded to fixed px values. It defaults to 9 px.

symbols.does: Converts a resolved density token into inner padding px.
symbols.description: Maps numeric density levels 1-5 to a fixed padding ladder and named tokens cozy/standard/airy to fixed values. It defaults to 11 px.

symbols.does: Normalizes a warmth value to a bounded number.
symbols.description: Parses the input as a float, clamps it between 0.2 and 1.4, and returns 0.7 when the input is not numeric. The result drives background gradient warmth.

symbols.does: Renders a theme/look specimen showing accent, radius, density, and warmth.
symbols.description: Builds a styled card with an accent bar, action and pick chips, and hairline lines, using normalized accent, radius, padding, and warmth values from resolved tokens.

symbols.does: Renders a palette specimen as a horizontal set of color stops.
symbols.description: Displays five vertical color swatches spanning base/accent, bronze, a warmth-tinted midtone, ink, and a translucent gold, arranged side by side.

symbols.does: Renders a typography specimen with a large glyph and text lines.
symbols.description: Shows a display-font Ag sample at a seeded font weight and size alongside two hairline bars. It ignores most resolved values except through the seed.

symbols.does: Renders a slide specimen in lead, center, or split layout.
symbols.description: Chooses a layout from resolved values or random selection and returns one of three distinct slide compositions: centered headline, split image/text, or leading headline with body lines.

symbols.does: Renders a deck specimen as stacked slide rectangles.
symbols.description: Reads the resolved slides count, clamps it between 2 and 4, and draws that many overlapping surface rectangles with gold-dashed borders and small shadows.

symbols.does: Renders a screen/surface specimen in wall, rail, or grid layout.
symbols.description: Uses resolved layout and density to produce a full-bleed thumbnail: a two-column grid, a rail with sidebar, or a wall with header and two content cards.

symbols.does: Renders a panel specimen as a bronze-tinted information block.
symbols.description: Draws a vertical stack of a short title bar and two hairline lines on a gold-50 background. It does not consume resolved values.

symbols.does: Renders a component specimen with a primary action chip.
symbols.description: Displays a title line, body line, and a resolved or seeded gold/quiet action chip, showing two possible component treatments.

symbols.does: Renders a toggle control specimen.
symbols.description: Draws an on/off segmented button where the active side is chosen by the seeded PRNG. It does not consume resolved values.

symbols.does: Renders a notification pill specimen.
symbols.description: Reads the resolved tone and maps it to a status color dot next to a short inverse line on a dark rounded pill. It defaults to info/gold when tone is missing.

symbols.does: Renders a widget/data-source specimen in one of several shapes.
symbols.description: Selects among single value, table rows, tree diagram, or sparkline chart based on resolved shape or random choice, producing distinct data previews.

symbols.does: Renders a simple connected-node diagram specimen.
symbols.description: Draws an SVG with two dashed connector lines and three nodes, where the first node is randomly a circle or rectangle. It is used for tree/flow/logic kinds.

symbols.does: Renders a flow/step specimen.
symbols.description: Draws a horizontal sequence of 3-4 rounded squares connected by short dashes, with the first step filled in gold. It is used for flow/walkthrough kinds.

symbols.does: Renders a motion/transition curve specimen.
symbols.description: Maps resolved curve names to SVG cubic-bezier path strings and draws the curve beneath a gold dot, falling back to a random curve when no curve is resolved.

symbols.does: Renders an image/illustration specimen with mood-driven gradients.
symbols.description: Builds a radial-plus-linear gradient background from warmth/mood and overlays two offset rounded rectangles to suggest layered imagery.

symbols.does: Renders a grid of icon specimens.
symbols.description: Renders six named Icon components in a 3-column grid with alternating bronze/gold ink colors. It relies on an external Icon component.

symbols.does: Renders a coachmark/tooltip guidance specimen.
symbols.description: Shows a ring target next to a small speech-bubble-like card containing two hairline lines. It is used for tutorial/walkthrough/tooltip kinds.

symbols.does: Renders a form/input specimen.
symbols.description: Draws 2-4 horizontal input rectangles with the last one shorter, using paper and gold-dashed borders. It does not consume resolved values.

symbols.does: Renders a chat/message specimen.
symbols.description: Draws three rounded message bubbles aligned left/right/left, alternating between surface and gold-50 backgrounds. It is used when form-like kinds are chat.
